page: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 1 physical core and 1 virtual CPU with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 4 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core test and Geekbench single-core score both placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression also performs strongly. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core benchmarks such as Geekbench multi-core, stress-ng multi-core, and Passmark CPU physics score fall into the bottom 10% to 25% categories. Memory read bandwidth is relatively weak, though large block write bandwidth remains average.

The c7a.medium does not include local NVMe storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. This resource profile presents a clear tradeoff: while single-threaded compute tasks benefit from the high-frequency AMD EPYC core and DDR5 memory, parallelized workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving are severely constrained by the single-core limit and low memory capacity.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c7a.medium offers a low-cost entry point to dedicated AMD Zen 4 architecture without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute workloads, basic text processing, simple single-threaded scripting, and small-scale development or testing tasks that require dedicated CPU execution but do not demand high memory or multi-core scaling.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized instance featuring a single dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 core running at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, with no local storage or baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for single-threaded stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks. However, its single-core, single-threaded design results in poor multi-core and database performance. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for users needing dedicated, high-frequency CPU execution for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and simple scripting, rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized instance featuring one dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC core, 2.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized instance with a single 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC core, 2.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated single-core compute instance featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing and DDR5 memory for efficient single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 1 vCPU and 1 physical core
2.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
No local storage and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic scripting, and testing
